title: Annual re-validation against updated WHO/UNICEF/CDC guidance
context: WASH guidance from WHO, UNICEF, and CDC is periodically updated. Content that was accurate at publication can become outdated or incorrect as official recommendations change. Annual re-validation ensures the content stays current. HIGH-risk units (dosing, ORS/dehydration, clinical) require a shorter cadence (e.g., as needed after guidance changes, minimum annually). This is a recurring maintenance task; the first instance establishes the process; subsequent instances are tracked separately.
objective: Conduct an annual re-validation of all published WashUnit content against current WHO/UNICEF/CDC guidance, update any content where official recommendations have changed, and re-trigger expert sign-off for any HIGH-risk unit whose content changes; produce a re-validation report committed to the repo.
acceptanceCriteria: All published WashUnit content is reviewed against the current versions of cited authoritative sources (WHO, UNICEF, CDC); any changes in official guidance since last review are identified

Any unit where authoritative guidance has materially changed is updated, re-reviewed, and (for HIGH-risk units) re-signed-off by the credentialed expert before re-publication; updated contentVersion, lastReviewed, and validUntil dates are set
HIGH-risk units are reviewed first and on a shorter cadence if guidance changes mid-year; any HIGH-risk unit past validUntil is withheld until re-validated and re-signed-off
A re-validation report is committed to the repo recording: which units were reviewed, which changed and how, which had expert re-sign-off, and the updated source retrieval dates
Re-validation is performed by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer; expert re-sign-off for HIGH-risk units is by the credentialed expert; both distinct from the re-validation author
output: A committed re-validation report documenting which units were reviewed, which were updated, and the updated lastReviewed/validUntil dates; any changed HIGH-risk units re-signed-off by the credentialed expert.
